narrativeOwner: Dekyi Liang, Chief of Staff & Vice President

assessment.headline: The March 9 Integrated SMT issued the most consequential directive batch since the university's founding — faculty cap, policy review, center consolidation, partnership formalization, and space review all in a single session. Execution is now the constraint: 2 directives are overdue, 1 is blocked on an external system upgrade, and several of the new structural directives have no defined methodology or timeline yet.
whereWeStand: The March 9 SMT produced the most consequential directive batch since MBZUAI's founding — faculty cap, policy review, center consolidation, partnership formalization, and space review all issued in a single session.

Goal setting submissions remain at ~72% compliance after missing the Feb 27 deadline. HR is following up with non-compliant departments. Workforce planning submissions are also incomplete — three divisions still outstanding.
Research admin ERP workflow alignment is blocked on a system upgrade not scheduled until April. This is the only externally blocked directive.
whatsNext: Policy review (all policies >3 years) and center consolidation are the largest directives in scope. Neither has a defined methodology or timeline yet.
Carlos joins full-time April 1 and will take ownership of partnership management. Reporting lines are not yet finalized.
IT application rationalization is a year-long initiative targeting 15–25% portfolio reduction. Approval process design is underway.

directives.title: HR benefits package rollout
directives.description: Announce improved compensation and benefits including enhanced educational allowances and college tuition support for faculty and staff.
directives.owner: Aamna Al Mansoori, ED Human Resources

updateHistory.note: Directive issued. Rollout planned for 6-9 months, ready for execution. Timing under review to avoid linking to crisis narrative.
Communication strategy finalized. Business-as-usual messaging. Virtual Q&A sessions planned.
Benefits announced to all staff. Q&A sessions completed. Sahab framework enabled for grading and benefits. 3-6 week stabilization support underway.

directives.title: Staff induction program V2
directives.description: Develop enhanced V2 of the staff orientation program incorporating experiential components based on V1 feedback (4.5+ average ratings).
directives.owner: Aamna Al Mansoori, ED Human Resources

updateHistory.note: V1 delivered with 26 participants and strong feedback. V2 planned for April with enhanced experiential components.
V2 curriculum in design. April delivery on track.

directives.title: 2026 goal setting submissions
directives.description: All staff to submit 2026 goal setting by deadline. SMT to reinforce deadline with teams.

updateHistory.note: Deadline set for Feb 27. General communications scheduled for Feb 19. SMT asked to reinforce with teams.
Deadline passed. Approximately 65% compliance. Multiple departments have not submitted.
Still at approximately 72% compliance. HR following up with non-compliant departments.

directives.title: Workforce planning — org charts and hiring plans
directives.description: All division leads to submit 2026/27 organizational charts and hiring plans.

updateHistory.note: March 9 deadline set for org charts and hiring plans.
Deadline reached. 4 of 8 divisions have submitted. Provost Office, Operations, NEO, and The Academy outstanding.
Provost Office submitted March 10. Three divisions still outstanding.

directives.title: Faculty hiring cap at 140 with new quality framework
directives.description: Limit total faculty to no more than 140 (current: ~128, max 12 new hires). Develop five-score evaluation framework targeting Top 30 US universities. Recalibrate hiring standards.
directives.owner: Prof. Thamar Solorio, Vice Provost of Faculty Excellence and Advancement / Provost Office

updateHistory.note: Directive issued at Integrated SMT. Cap set at 140. Five-score evaluation framework to be developed. All new hires to meet Top 30 US university caliber standard.
Framework design initiated. Division deans to be briefed before any new hiring proceeds. Several pipeline candidates being reassessed against new standard.

directives.title: Comprehensive policy review (all policies >3 years)
directives.description: All policies older than three years to be revisited for relevance and updated as needed. University-wide effort.
directives.owner: Susan Wei, Associate Provost / All Division Leads

updateHistory.note: Directive issued at Integrated SMT. No start date or methodology defined yet. IEQA team tasked with auditing policy relevance and maintaining public policy webpage.

directives.title: Space utilization review
directives.description: Conduct full review of space occupancy and needs. Prioritize academic space (faculty and students) over administrative use. Faculty who claim space but do not use it will be challenged.

updateHistory.note: Directive issued. Full review of space occupancy and needs to be conducted. Flexible approach to space assignment encouraged. Rooftop access locked down due to recent safety incident.
Operations team beginning space audit. Preliminary data collection on occupancy rates started.

directives.title: Center and partnership consolidation review
directives.description: Consolidate proposals for new centers and review by committee. Assess how many centers to establish and alignment with university goals. Formalize partnership management with clear ownership.
directives.owner: Provost Office / Carlos (upon full-time start April 1)

updateHistory.note: Directive issued. Currently ad hoc — no clear ownership of partnership management. Carlos joining full-time April 1 and relocating July. Reporting lines yet to be finalized.
Preliminary center inventory being compiled by Strategy team. Partnership management ownership discussion scheduled for April once Carlos is full-time.

directives.title: IT application rationalization
directives.description: Implement standardized approval process for IT applications. Enterprise-wide rationalization. Target 15-25% portfolio reduction in 2026. Publish target-state architecture. Lightweight tiered approval process (<5 days turnaround).

updateHistory.note: Benchmarking revealed 83 applications, 40+ vendors, no formal governance. Key risks: functional duplication, multiple systems of record, rising demand exceeding capacity, limited cost visibility.
Standardized approval process design underway. UG application portfolio audit initiated. Target-state architecture drafting to begin in April.

directives.title: New faculty offer letters with revised packages
directives.description: Update faculty offer letters with revised startup funding and student cost details. Disseminate standardized letters and communicate funding policy changes to candidates.

updateHistory.note: Current offer letters reflect outdated packages. New letters to include updated startup funding and student cost details. March dissemination target.
Draft revised offer letter template under review by Provost and HR. Legal review pending. On track for late March.

directives.title: Student funding and matching policy
directives.description: Formulate clear policies on student funding, matching, and performance expectations. Starting with 2025 cohort, second-year master's students become financial responsibility of matched faculty advisor.

updateHistory.note: Need for clear guidelines on timing and criteria for matching students to faculty. Concerns about financial burden from retaining underperforming students. Junior faculty equity issues flagged.
Provost Office drafting policy document. Consultation with division deans scheduled for mid-March.

directives.title: Website Phase 1 delivery
directives.description: Deliver Phase 1 of the new MBZUAI website.

updateHistory.note: Phase 1 on track for May delivery. Platform decision pending.
Development progressing. Platform decision still pending confirmation from IT/SMT.

directives.title: Research admin workflows aligned with ERP
directives.description: Align research administration workflows with ERP financial reporting for better automation.
directives.owner: Prof. Ian Reid, Acting Director of Research Administration

updateHistory.note: Target: March automation. Coordination with finance, legal, and research teams underway.
Blocked — ERP system upgrade dependency. Finance team reports upgrade not scheduled until April. Workaround being explored but full automation delayed.
